latest.title: Dolphins use signature whistles like names

latest.title: Dogs bond with humans the same way we do
latest.script_key: watch this one twice that little butt wiggle isn t just cute dogs release oxytocin when they gaze at us the same chemical that bonds parents to babies they re literally hardwired to bond that s why your rescue pup knows you re family before you even open the bag would your dog pass the stare down test

latest.title: Squirrels memorize every nut they bury

latest.title: Golden retrievers tilt heads for a real reason
latest.script_key: watch their heads tilt every time you speak they re not just cute that tilt scoops up sound waves like satellite dishes the left ear picks up high pitch faster so they cock right to balance the delay that s why they seem to lean in did you know humans do this too when we can t hear

latest.title: Whales keep the ocean dark green
latest.script_key: watch the whale s tail that dark green isn t just water it s floating life whales poop near the surface and that waste fertilizes plankton blooms because plankton stores carbon every whale is a tiny climate machine did you learn they just swim around

latest.title: Camels don't store water in their humps
latest.script_key: here s the part nobody tells you camel humps are fat reserves not water storage that s why they shrink when food s scarce watch the hump sway as they walk those two bumps are pure energy for the next desert sprint did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Crows remember faces for years
latest.script_key: that crow just stared you down notice how it tilts its head side to side crows can remember human faces for years and pass that intel to their flock they re not just being nosy that s their survival tool so next time you meet one give a little nod what s the wildest thing it ll remember about you

latest.title: Desert views hide a mountain's secret
